source_document: USAA Property Direct Repair Program Guidelines and Consistencies

disallowed_items.description: Inspection fees not permissible unless pre-approved
disallowed_items.reason: Unless inspection is requested by USAA and inspection fee is preapproved, inspection fees are not permissible under the USAA Program

quantity_limits.description: Waste for carpet and vinyl: 15% maximum
quantity_limits.reason: Waste is built into the Xactimate unit cost for all flooring line items except carpet and sheet vinyl. All carpet and vinyl replacement estimates should be calculated using the cut method or applying 15% waste

quantity_limits.description: Scaffolding only if platform exceeds 20 feet
quantity_limits.reason: Generally, a contractor will have and use standard ladders, jacks, and plank scaffolding as part of their normal equipment toolset. The labor yield assumption addresses the average time necessary for normal one- or two-story applications, including the set up and use of this equipment as needed, where the ladder scaffold platform does not exceed 20'

overhead_profit_restrictions.reason: Non-cumulative 10% overhead & 10% profit is allowed provided: There are multiple approved/recognized skilled trades represented in the estimate and there is a reasonable expectation that the services of a General Contractor are necessary to coordinate the repairs
Please note that there are certain states/counties where 15% overhead and 10% profit are afforded at the loss location

f9_note_requirements.description: F9 note required for partial repairs/replacements
f9_note_requirements.reason: All PDRP contractors will place an F9 NOTE on the applicable line item(s) indicating that line item & measurement is for a partial repair/replacement ONLY
f9_note_requirements.required_info: Indicate which slope/area/room/elevation where damage is located
f9_note_requirements.recommended_note: This line item is for partial repair/replacement ONLY. Damage located at [specify location/area].

f9_note_requirements.description: F9 note required for all manual minimum charges
f9_note_requirements.reason: All manually entered Minimum Charges must have an 'F9' note attached to clarify what the line item includes
f9_note_requirements.required_info: Clarify what work the minimum charge includes
f9_note_requirements.recommended_note: Minimum charge for [trade] includes: [list specific work performed].

f9_note_requirements.description: F9 note required for additional labor hours
f9_note_requirements.reason: Labor cost is included in most line-item cost in Xactimate. Labor hours to supplement line-item cost are not permissible. If there is an exception that renders the unit cost in Xactimate ineffective, the additional labor cost must be supported with photos and documentation in XactAnalysis Notes; including F9 Xactimate note
f9_note_requirements.required_info: Explain why Xactimate unit cost is ineffective and why additional labor is needed
f9_note_requirements.recommended_note: Additional labor required due to [specific condition]. Xactimate line item does not account for [explain situation]. See photos for documentation.

f9_note_requirements.description: F9 note required for additional masking beyond standard
f9_note_requirements.reason: If additional masking line items are used for drywall work, demo, etc., provide F9 note for explanation
f9_note_requirements.required_info: Explain why additional masking is required beyond standard painting work
f9_note_requirements.recommended_note: Additional masking required due to [reason - e.g., extensive drywall work, demolition, overspray protection]. See photos.

f9_note_requirements.description: F9 note required when detaching/resetting undamaged plumbing
f9_note_requirements.reason: Plumbing components (supply lines, p-traps, fixtures, etc.) are typically reusable and may not require resetting when undamaged by the covered peril. Allowance to replace or detach/reset undamaged components should be documented (photos and F9 note) when not evident by the repair methodology
f9_note_requirements.required_info: Explain why undamaged component needs to be detached/reset or replaced
f9_note_requirements.recommended_note: Undamaged plumbing component requires detach/reset due to [reason - e.g., access for repairs, age/condition makes reuse not feasible]. See photos.

f9_note_requirements.description: F9 note required for temporary repair methods
f9_note_requirements.reason: Photos and F9 notes should clearly support methods employed for temporary repairs
f9_note_requirements.required_info: Document the temporary repair method and materials used
f9_note_requirements.recommended_note: Temporary repair using [method/materials] to prevent further damage until permanent repairs can be completed. See photos.

f9_note_requirements.description: F9 note required for mitigation equipment setup costs
f9_note_requirements.reason: Cost for initial set up is permissible; based on amount of equipment needed and should be documented with F9 notes
f9_note_requirements.required_info: Document the type and amount of equipment set up
f9_note_requirements.recommended_note: Initial setup for [number] dehumidifiers, [number] air movers, and [other equipment] to dry affected areas. See equipment log.

f9_note_requirements.description: F9 note required when replacing only carpet pad
f9_note_requirements.reason: If contractor is replacing carpet pad when only the carpet is damaged (no damage to existing carpet pad), they should support the recommendation in XactAnalysis Notes
f9_note_requirements.required_info: Age of carpet/pad, traffic level, and uniform appearance assessment
f9_note_requirements.recommended_note: Carpet pad replacement recommended. Carpet is [age] years old in high-traffic area. Replacing only carpet without pad would result in non-uniform appearance due to [reason].

observations.description: Semi-gloss and high gloss trim should not be painted if undamaged
observations.reason: Semi-Gloss and High Gloss painted baseboards, door, window, and door trim, etc. should not be painted if not damaged
observations.recommendation: Review if undamaged trim is included in paint scope

observations.description: Check for natural break points in flooring
observations.reason: If a closing door (carpet only), or threshold separates the room or the run is not continuous into the next room, replacement of other undamaged areas should not be included
observations.recommendation: Verify flooring scope stops at natural break points (thresholds, closing doors)

observations.description: Sealant typically only for new drywall, stains, or areas to be textured
observations.reason: Sealant is typically applied for new drywall, stained areas, or areas to be textured
observations.recommendation: Verify sealant is only applied where necessary

observations.description: Waste is built into most flooring items except carpet/vinyl
observations.reason: Waste is built into the Xactimate unit cost for all flooring line item except carpet and sheet vinyl
observations.recommendation: Verify waste is not added to flooring items other than carpet and vinyl

notes: All estimates should 'speak for themselves' with photos, F9 notes, claims documentation, and/or XactAnalysis Notes
Line items within the same category should not be added to a minimum charge
Each claim should be evaluated on its own merit for matching and appearance considerations
XactAnalysis Notes should document all decisions and member communications
